title: User Request
description: Verbatim record of the user's original instruction(s) that started this project. Written at project init, before any stage runs. The canonical source of truth for user intent — every downstream artifact (intake_brief, brief, research_brief, etc.) is an interpretation of this. Never edited or rewritten; revisions are appended as additional turns.

created_at.description: ISO 8601 timestamp of when the request was first captured.

prompt.description: Verbatim text of the user's first actionable instruction. Stored exactly as written — no paraphrasing, no cleanup, no translation.

prompt_turns.description: Subsequent verbatim user messages that materially refined the brief during intake. Append-only; do not edit prior entries.

note.description: Optional one-line agent annotation explaining why this turn matters (e.g., 'changed target platform', 'added reference video').

references.description: Concrete materials the user pointed to in their request — URLs, local file paths, music_library tracks. Captured verbatim; not yet validated.

pipeline_hint.description: Pipeline name if the user explicitly named one (e.g., 'cinematic', 'screen-demo'). Null when the agent must infer.

session_id.description: Optional Claude Code (or other harness) session id pointing back to the originating transcript. Best-effort; not required.

language.description: BCP 47 tag of the prompt language, e.g. 'en', 'zh-CN'. Optional but useful for downstream localization decisions.

metadata.description: Free-form bag for harness-specific context (operator name, channel, ticket id, etc.).
